forest_arrival.text: The trees began where the path ended. They weren't growing — they were standing still, the way someone stands when they've forgotten why they walked into a room.

forest_arrival.speaker: Sable
forest_arrival.text: This is the Memory Forest. The trees are what's left of the first settlement near BL-07.

forest_arrival.speaker: Tobias
forest_arrival.text: A settlement? There's no record of habitation this close to a Void Hole.

forest_arrival.speaker: Sable
forest_arrival.text: That's the point. BL-07 ate the records. Ate the people. The trees are what grew in the gap.
She was right. The bark was body-temperature. Like pressing your hand against sleeping skin.

forest_arrival.speaker: Sable
forest_arrival.text: Don't touch them for long. They'll take what you're feeling and replace it with what they remember.

forest_arrival.speaker: Sable
forest_arrival.text: Walk fast. Say your name if the edges go soft.

ghost_encounter.text: A shape between the trees. Not shadow — presence. A woman standing perfectly still, her mouth open in a word that had no sound.

ghost_encounter.speaker: Sable
ghost_encounter.text: A remnant. What's left when BL-07 takes everything except the shape.

ghost_encounter.speaker: Tobias
ghost_encounter.text: She's trying to speak. The jaw moves but there's nothing to say — the words were consumed.

ghost_encounter.speaker: Sable
ghost_encounter.text: She's not suffering. She can't. Suffering requires memory of not suffering.

ghost_encounter.speaker: Sable
ghost_encounter.text: The kindest thing is to walk past. Don't give her your attention — it feeds the forest.
They walked past. The shape's mouth kept moving. Arrel didn't look back.

tobias_theory.speaker: Tobias
tobias_theory.text: I have a theory. The forest isn't random — it's organized.

tobias_theory.speaker: Tobias
tobias_theory.text: These trees are arranged in concentric rings. The oldest are at the center, closest to BL-07.

tobias_theory.speaker: Arrel
tobias_theory.text: Ring growth. Like a real forest.

tobias_theory.speaker: Tobias
tobias_theory.text: Exactly. But these rings correspond to consumption events. Each ring is a wave of memories eaten.

tobias_theory.speaker: Tobias
tobias_theory.text: If my count is correct... BL-07 has had seventeen major feeding events in the last forty years.

tobias_theory.speaker: Sable
tobias_theory.text: Eighteen. You missed the one that took my team.

elia_anchor_strain.speaker: Elia
elia_anchor_strain.text: You just called me 'Elise.' Three times.

elia_anchor_strain.speaker: Arrel
elia_anchor_strain.text: I... no, I said Elia. I'm sure I—

elia_anchor_strain.speaker: Elia
elia_anchor_strain.text: The forest is working on you. Hold still.
She took his hands again. The anchoring was harder here — like trying to hold a page flat in a gale.

elia_anchor_strain.speaker: Elia
elia_anchor_strain.text: Think of something real. Something that happened.
The edges sharpened. The name returned. But something else — something small and unnamed — slipped away in its place.

forest_whispers.text: The deeper they went, the louder the forest became. Not sound — pressure. Like the air was trying to speak.

forest_whispers.speaker: Sable
forest_whispers.text: It's getting denser. We're approaching the inner rings.

forest_whispers.speaker: Tobias
forest_whispers.text: My pen stopped working three trees ago. I'm writing with charcoal now.
Something whispered Arrel's name. Not from a direction — from inside his own memory of hearing his name.

forest_whispers.speaker: Sable
forest_whispers.text: Don't. Don't finish that sentence. Whatever you heard, it wasn't real.

forest_whispers.speaker: Sable
forest_whispers.text: The forest uses your own memories as bait. It plays back what you expect to hear.

forest_whispers.speaker: Elia
forest_whispers.text: Then how do we know any of us are real right now?

forest_whispers.speaker: Sable
forest_whispers.text: You don't. That's why you keep walking.

clearing_rest.text: A clearing. The trees pulled back as if repelled by something in the soil. In the center, a cairn of white stones.

clearing_rest.speaker: Tobias
clearing_rest.text: Bureau markers. Standard expedition cairn — someone made it this deep and turned back.

clearing_rest.speaker: Sable
clearing_rest.text: My team. We built this on the third expedition. It was as far as we got before—
She stopped. For the first time, her composure cracked — just a fissure, quickly sealed.

clearing_rest.speaker: Sable
clearing_rest.text: Rest here. The cairn creates a dead zone. The forest can't reach this spot.

clearing_rest.speaker: Sable
clearing_rest.text: Because these stones are from outside BL-07's range. They remember being stones. The forest doesn't know what to do with certainty.

clearing_rest.speaker: Tobias
clearing_rest.text: Fascinating. Memory-null objects as anchoring points. I need to document this.

clearing_rest.speaker: Sable
clearing_rest.text: Use charcoal. Ink is dead here.

forest_departure.speaker: Sable
forest_departure.text: Beyond the forest, the color drains. Completely.

forest_departure.speaker: Sable
forest_departure.text: Not gray — absence. The Waste is where even the memory of color was eaten.

forest_departure.speaker: Tobias
forest_departure.text: The Bureau calls it the Achromatic Zone. No expedition has mapped it.

forest_departure.speaker: Arrel
forest_departure.text: How close are we to BL-07?

forest_departure.speaker: Sable
forest_departure.text: Half a day's walk. If we survive the Waste.
They left the cairn behind. The forest closed in one last time, then — abruptly — ended. Ahead: nothing with color.

forest_atmosphere.text: The ground was soft. Not with moss — with something that used to be moss before it forgot what growing meant.

forest_atmosphere.speaker: Tobias
forest_atmosphere.text: The air composition here is abnormal. It smells like old paper.

forest_atmosphere.speaker: Elia
forest_atmosphere.text: Like a library left open in the rain.

forest_atmosphere.speaker: Tobias
forest_atmosphere.text: Precisely. Memory residue has a scent — oxidized narrative, if you will.

ghost_child.text: A smaller shape this time. Child-sized. It held something — or held the memory of holding something. Its hands were cupped around absence.

ghost_child.speaker: Sable
ghost_child.text: Don't. Age doesn't matter here. They're all the same age now — zero.
Arrel held it to his ear. A child's voice, clearer than the others: 'I had a dog. His name was— his name was—'
The sentence looped. The name was gone. Just the shape of having known it remained.

ghost_child.speaker: Arrel
ghost_child.text: ...we have to seal BL-07. Whatever it takes.

tobias_ring_theory.speaker: Tobias
tobias_ring_theory.text: I've been mapping the rings. Each one corresponds to a consumption event — but the spacing isn't random.

tobias_ring_theory.speaker: Tobias
tobias_ring_theory.text: The intervals are decreasing. Ring one to ring two: eight years. Ring sixteen to seventeen: four months. It's accelerating.

tobias_ring_theory.speaker: Arrel
tobias_ring_theory.text: What does that mean for The Seam?

tobias_ring_theory.speaker: Tobias
tobias_ring_theory.text: It means BL-07 isn't growing steadily. It's growing like hunger — the more it eats, the faster it needs to eat again. My Ring Theory predicts total collapse within a year.

ghost_mother.text: A figure stood at the edge of the clearing. A woman, translucent, her arms cradling something invisible. Her mouth shaped a single word over and over.

ghost_mother.speaker: Elia
ghost_mother.text: She's looking for someone. A child. You can see it in the way her arms are held — muscle memory outlasting everything else.
The ghost turned toward them. For one terrible moment, recognition flickered in her hollow eyes — then faded. She drifted back among the trees, still searching.

forest_heart.text: The air thickened. The trees leaned inward, their bark pulsing with a rhythm like slow breathing.

forest_heart.speaker: Tobias
forest_heart.text: We've reached it. The parasitic core. Every tree in this forest is connected to this point — a single organism feeding on memory residue.

forest_heart.speaker: Sable
forest_heart.text: It's not just feeding. It's growing. Last time I was here, the core was half this size.

arrel_flashback_elia.text: The forest whispers a memory you'd almost lost.
A girl standing in the rain. Translucent. Not quite real.
That was the moment everything changed.
